steps.title: Find where circle A meets the x-axis
steps.content: Circle A: $(x+8)^2+(y-8)^2=64$ has center $(-8,8)$ and radius $8$. Set $y=0$:$$(x+8)^2+(0-8)^2=64\Rightarrow (x+8)^2=0\Rightarrow x=-8$$So $(r,s)=(-8,0)$.

steps.title: Write the equation for circle B
steps.content: Circle B is centered at $(-8,0)$ with radius $8$. Standard form $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$ gives:$$(x+8)^2+y^2=64$$

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A) $x^2+(y+8)^2=64$: center $(0,-8)$, not $(-8,0)$.
  • B) $x^2+(y-8)^2=64$: center $(0,8)$, not $(-8,0)$.
  • C) $(x+8)^2+y^2=64$: center $(-8,0)$, radius $8$. Matches.
  • D) $(x-8)^2+y^2=64$: center $(8,0)$, not $(-8,0)$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
